title: sedum anglicum subsp. arenarium
intro: Sedum anglicum subsp. arenarium, also known as the coastal stonecrop, is a subspecies of the sedum anglicum plant, a type of succulent that is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of sedum anglicum subsp. arenarium include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infection, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
